WeeklySchedule.title: WeeklySchedule
WeeklySchedule.description: Represents a single entry in the Bishops Hull Hub's weekly activity schedule, providing details like activity name, time, and contact information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeeklySchedule entity.

activityName.description: The name of the activity or class (e.g., 'Rich Parkers Fitness').

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week when the activity takes place (e.g., 'Monday', 'Tuesday').

startTime.description: The scheduled start time of the activity in HH:MM format (e.g., '06:30').

endTime.description: The scheduled end time of the activity in HH:MM format (e.g., '07:00').

category.description: A category for the activity (e.g., 'Fitness', 'Youth Club', 'Community').

contactInfo.description: Contact information (e.g., email address or URL) for the activity organizer or instructor.

frequency.description: Describes how often the activity occurs (e.g., 'Weekly', 'Fortnightly', 'First Tuesday of the Month').

description.description: A brief description of the activity, including target audience or special notes.

BookingEnquiry.title: BookingEnquiry
BookingEnquiry.description: Stores information submitted through the 'Hire the Hub' booking enquiry form, used for administrative review and communication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookingEnquiry entity.

name.description: The full name of the person submitting the enquiry.

emailAddress.description: The email address of the enquirer for follow-up communication.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the enquirer.

dateRequired.description: The specific date or range of dates requested for the booking.

startTime.description: The requested start time for the booking in HH:MM format.

endTime.description: The requested end time for the booking in HH:MM format.

typeOfEvent.description: A description of the type of event or purpose of the booking (e.g., 'Birthday Party', 'Community Meeting').

estimatedAttendance.description: The estimated number of attendees for the event.

additionalRequirements.description: Any additional requirements or special requests from the enquirer.

submissionDateTime.description: The timestamp when the booking enquiry was submitted.

status.description: The current processing status of the booking enquiry (e.g., 'Pending', 'Reviewed', 'Confirmed', 'Rejected').

CommunityInitiative.title: CommunityInitiative
CommunityInitiative.description: Defines a community project or initiative, including its details, call-to-action, and links.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CommunityInitiative entity.

title.description: The main title of the community initiative (e.g., 'Join the 100 Club', 'Sensory Trail').

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the initiative, used for navigation (e.g., 'join-100-club').

description.description: The full explanatory text detailing the purpose and information about the initiative.

callToActionText.description: The text displayed on a call-to-action button or link related to the initiative (e.g., 'Learn More', 'Donate Now').

callToActionLink.description: The URL or internal path associated with the call-to-action (e.g., an external payment portal, an internal form).

displayOrder.description: An integer defining the display order of the initiative on the website, allowing for custom sorting.

GalleryImage.title: GalleryImage
GalleryImage.description: Stores metadata for images used in galleries, such as the 'Community Garden Gallery', referencing their location in cloud storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GalleryImage entity.

storagePath.description: The path or URL to the image file stored in Firebase Cloud Storage (e.g., 'community-garden/summer_view.webp').

altText.description: Alternative text for the image, crucial for accessibility and SEO.

caption.description: A short caption or description to be displayed with the image.

category.description: A category to group related images, allowing for multiple galleries (e.g., 'Community Garden', 'Hall Interior').

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the image was uploaded.

displayOrder.description: An integer defining the display order of the image within its category or gallery.

PageContent.title: PageContent
PageContent.description: Manages static content for various pages or sections of the website, enabling updates without code changes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PageContent entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the content page or section (e.g., 'hire-prices', 'privacy-statement').

title.description: The main title of the content page or section (e.g., 'Hire Prices', 'Privacy Statement').

content.description: The main body text or rich HTML content for the page or section.

lastUpdated.description: The timestamp indicating when the content was last updated.

category.description: A category to group related content, such as 'Legal', 'Hire Information', 'About Us'.

definition.description: Stores individual weekly activity schedule entries. Documents are publicly readable to display the timetable and are admin-editable. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is public read and role-based admin write (via /admins collection).

Holds booking enquiry submissions from the 'Hire the Hub' form. Documents are created by any user (anonymous or authenticated) and are readable only by administrators. Includes a 'status' field for explicit state management. No denormalized authorization fields are required as submission is public and read is role-based admin (via /admins collection).

params.name: enquiryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific booking enquiry.

definition.description: Contains details for various community projects and initiatives. Documents are publicly readable for display on the website and are admin-editable. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is public read and role-based admin write (via /admins collection).

params.name: initiativeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific community initiative.

definition.description: Stores metadata for images displayed in galleries (e.g., Community Garden Gallery). Documents are publicly readable and are admin-editable. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is public read and role-based admin write (via /admins collection).

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific gallery image.

definition.description: Manages static content for various pages or sections of the website. Documents are publicly readable and are admin-editable. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is public read and role-based admin write (via /admins collection).

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific piece of page content.

definition.description: A collection storing the user IDs (UIDs) of authenticated administrators. The mere existence of a document in this collection with a user's UID as its ID grants administrative privileges across the application. This collection facilitates DBAC (Database Access Control) without relying on custom claims or hierarchical reads for authorization in other collections.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes clarity, security, and scalability by adhering to the core design principles. Each primary entity is mapped to a top-level collection, ensuring structural segregation where all documents within a collection share a homogeneous security posture. 

Authorization Independence is achieved primarily by having public-facing collections (`weekly_schedules`, `community_initiatives`, `gallery_images`, `page_contents`) that are universally readable, removing any need for hierarchical authorization lookups. For administrative write access to these collections, and read/write access to `booking_enquiries`, a separate top-level `/admins/{adminId}` collection is used for role-based access control. The existence of `request.auth.uid` in this collection will grant admin privileges, avoiding `get()` calls on parent documents for authorization decisions, thus maintaining authorization independence and supporting atomic operations. For example, an admin can update a `WeeklySchedule` document without needing to fetch any other document to verify their permissions.

QAPs (Query as a Policy) are supported through this design. For public collections, security rules will simply allow reads (`if true`), meaning any query will return all matching documents without implicitly filtering based on permissions. For the `booking_enquiries` collection, only authenticated administrators (verified via the `/admins/{adminId}` collection) will have read access. A query for `booking_enquiries` by an admin will therefore retrieve all documents, as intended by the administrator's role, without filtering. Non-admins will be explicitly denied read access, preventing unauthorized queries from returning partial or no data unexpectedly. The `BookingEnquiry` entity itself is designed to be self-contained, including all necessary submission details and a `status` field for explicit state modeling, further simplifying rules and ensuring data integrity.
